Cancer Incidence Projections Under Standard and Intended-Effect Trial Designs

Kemal Caglar Gogebakan

2026-03-20

Scenario

This section defines the trial design, test performance, and number of participants in each arm.
Users can change these inputs and rerun the file to explore alternative designs.

Parameter Value
Cancer type lung
OMST (years) 3.5
LMST (years) 1
Early-stage sensitivity 20%
Late-stage sensitivity 95%
Screening interval Annual
Number of screens 10
Follow-up years 1
Starting age 66
Number of participants (Control arm) 1e+05
Number of participants (Screen arm) 1e+05

Run Main Projection Function

This chunk runs the main projection function once and stores all trial results for: - both designs (standard, IE) - both arms (control, screen) - all stages (early, late, overall) - stage shift

User Output Selection

The tables and figures below are generated for one selected output format.
Users can rerun the file with different settings to obtain alternative yearly or cumulative results, and incidence or count outputs.

Selected output settings
Setting Value
Yearly or Cumulative cumulative
Outcome measure count
Show early-stage results Yes
Show overall results Yes

Standard Trial Design Projections

This section presents results under the standard trial design.
The first table always shows late-stage results and stage shift.
If selected above, separate early-stage and overall tables and figures are also shown.

Figures for the Standard Trial Design

# Intended-Effect Trial Design Projections

This section presents parallel results under the intended-effect design.
A positivity-rate table is shown first because intended-effect results are conditional on ever-positive status.

Positivity rate

Positivity rate at the end of the screening period
Measure Value
Early-stage screen-positive fraction 0.9%
Late-stage screen-positive fraction 0.7%
Overall screen-positive fraction 1.6%

Figures for the Intended Effect Design

Per-arm / total sample size and achieved power

This section computes: - the required total sample size for the target power - the achieved power under the current sample sizes - for both the standard and intended-effect designs

Power calculation inputs
Parameter Value
Significance level (alpha) 0.025
Target power (%) 80
Number in control arm 1e+05
Number in screen arm 1e+05
Total sample size 2e+05
Allocation ratio (R = n_control / n_screen) 1
Required total sample size and achieved power under each trial design (current total N = 2e+05)
Trial_Design Cumulative_incidence_control_arm Cumulative_incidence_screen_arm Required_total_sample_size Power_under_current_design
Standard 0.0151830 0.0111636 25263 100
Intended-Effect 0.6629602 0.4272187 8529 100
Current allocation, positivity rate, and sample-size ratio
Measure Value
Number of participants in control arm 1e+05
Number of participants in screen arm 1e+05
Number of participants in the trial 2e+05
Allocation ratio R = n_control / n_screen 1
Positivity rate 0.0164
Required N ratio: IE / Standard 0.34

Interpretation

  • Standard design results are population-level projections.
  • Intended-effect design results are conditional on ever-positive individuals.
  • To explore alternative projections or sample-size requirements, users can change:
    • number of screens
    • screening interval
    • follow-up duration
    • test sensitivities
